eventName: Prompt Engineering vs Context Engineering: What Actually Works
description: An evening of talks exploring the shift from prompt engineering to context engineering. We'll compare approaches, share real-world results, and debate what matters most when building AI-powered applications.
targetAudience: Developers building with LLM APIs who want to move beyond basic prompting
prerequisites: Familiarity with at least one AI framework

suggestedSpeakers.name: Marcus Johnson
suggestedSpeakers.reason: Prompt engineering is one of his core topics. His DevRel background means he can make the comparison accessible to all levels.

suggestedSpeakers.name: Elena Vasquez
suggestedSpeakers.reason: Her AI UX and product design perspective adds a practical 'what users actually need' angle to the engineering debate.

suggestedSpeakers.name: Alex Rivera
suggestedSpeakers.reason: Can ground the discussion in production reality — what actually works when you ship AI to real users at scale.

suggestedVenue.name: Downtown Tech Hub
suggestedVenue.reason: Capacity of 80 comfortably fits 55 attendees. Mic system and projector are ideal for a talk format. Central location with good transit access.

slots.title: Context Engineering: Designing What the Model Sees
slots.speaker: elena-vasquez
